review: Kicking myself that I didn’t beg or borrow a Blu-ray when I screened this for my film students this week, but the image quality didn’t seem to hinder their enjoyment much: truly a delight to watch it with them. review: A father dies to protect his son. The son surrenders to save his apprentice. A friend cripples himself to avenge the son. review: Ti Lung and Chow Yun-fat are both great, the plot is fun and there are few incredible scenes, but overal nothing was really unforgettable.
